title: Raury & Tray Wellington — Live at Eddie's Attic
description: Genre-blending indie soul/folk at Eddie's Attic, Decatur. Raury is a Grammy-nominated Atlanta artist known for blending hip-hop, soul, and folk influences. Tray Wellington brings jazz andexperimental fire. Great lineup for a date — intimate room, serious artists, and Decatur's walkable brewery scene nearby for before/after. Saturday May 23, show starts 8 PM.
location: Eddie's Attic, 515-B E Howard Ave, Decatur, GA
vibe: Intimate, soulful, creative — perfect for music lovers who want a real performance

title: Amy Sherald: American Sublime — High Museum of Art
description: Amy Sherald's largest exhibition ever — the Baltimore-born, Atlanta-based painter known for her luminous portraits of everyday Black life. 'American Sublime' features 50+ works spanning her entire career. Dress code: casual cool. Walk the exhibit together, grab coffee at the museum café, then wander the Peckitt Corner galleries nearby. Runs through August so it's flexible, but this weekend is prime.
location: High Museum of Art, 1280 Peachtree St NE, Atlanta
vibe: Sophisticated, thought-provoking, conversation-starting — great for first dates or regular date nights

title: Life Bistro Vegan Brunch — Best Plant-Based in ATL
description: Atlanta's top-rated vegan brunch spot. Their Chick'n & Waffles (spelt waffle + fried portobello) and the brunch bowls are legendary. Weekend hours 11 AM–3 PM. Go Saturday or Sunday around noon — the vibe is relaxed, the food is consistently great, and the portions are shareable. Lots of outdoor seating options nearby if you want to make a whole afternoon of it.
location: Life Bistro, 5496 Bullsboro Dr #10, Atlanta (Powers Ferry area)
vibe: Fresh, relaxed, health-conscious — for couples who love good food without the meat默认
